segments.text: And Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him.
And when Jacob saw them,
This is God's host:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: and he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

footnotes: Mahanaim: that is, Two hosts, or, camps

segments.text: And Jacob sent messengers before him to Esau his brother unto the land of Seir, the country of Edom.
And he commanded them, saying,
Thus shall ye speak unto my lord Esau; Thy servant Jacob saith thus, I have sojourned with Laban, and stayed there until now:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And I have oxen, and asses, flocks, and menservants, and womenservants: and I have sent to tell my lord, that I may find grace in thy sight.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And the messengers returned to Jacob, saying,
We came to thy brother Esau, and also he cometh to meet thee, and four hundred men with him.
segments.speaker: Messengers

segments.text: Then Jacob was greatly afraid and distressed: and he divided the people that was with him, and the flocks, and herds, and the camels, into two bands;
And said,
If Esau come to the one company, and smite it, then the other company which is left shall escape.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: O God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac, the LORD which saidst unto me, Return unto thy country, and to thy kindred, and I will deal well with thee:
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: I am not worthy of the least of all the mercies, and of all the truth, which thou hast shewed unto thy servant; for with my staff I passed over this Jordan; and now I am become two bands.
segments.speaker: Jacob

footnotes: I am not…: Heb. I am less than all

segments.text: Deliver me, I pray thee, from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau: for I fear him, lest he will come and smite me, and the mother with the children.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And thou saidst, I will surely do thee good, and make thy seed as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And Jacob was left alone; and there wrestled a man with him until the breaking of the day.

footnotes: breaking…: Heb. ascending of the morning
heading: Jacob Wrestles with God

segments.text: Let me go, for the day breaketh.
segments.speaker: Mysterious Man

segments.text: I will not let thee go, except thou bless me.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Thy name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel: for as a prince hast thou power with God and with men, and hast prevailed.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: Israel: that is, A prince of God

segments.text: And Jacob called the name of the place Peniel:
for I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.
segments.speaker: Jacob

footnotes: Peniel: that is, The face of God
